title: Update-replies as threads
excerpt:

posts.author: myjos (My)
posts.cookedHtml:
When a new update is created and replied to the first time - it is somewhat easy to see what it is for.

But it starts to get messy fast as the replies continues on the same update. When clicking on reply on another reply it would be nice if its not just an @ that is added - but that the reply is nested below the reply that you actually are replying to. Now they just get added below eachother, so it is hard to know which reply is replied to.

Messy to explain

Instead of this:

  • Update
  • Reply to update
  • Another reply
  • Reply to “reply to update” but its not apparent

It would be:

  • Update
  • Reply to update
    ---- Reply to “reply to update” where it is nested under the reply you actually replied to
  • Another reply

posts.author: fabiozampieri (fabio zampieri)
posts.cookedHtml: would be really helpful. each response should be nested to the source comment.

posts.author: JessWinn09 (Jessica Winn)
posts.cookedHtml:
This is actually VERY annoying and I don’t understand why this is a feature that needs to be requested in 2024. Conversations with multiple people working on big projects start to confuse which reply is for who. Can this PLEASE be fixed? Also why have ‘REPLY’ under each message like you’re able to nest comment under it when all it does is drop comment to bottom of thread?
